thesis: Trinity Place Holdings Inc. is a Steel Partners-controlled, non-operating shell: no operating business after the May 2025 TPHGreenwich Trust transfer, about $54 thousand of cash, a small legacy IP licensing trickle, and roughly $330 million of federal and state net operating loss carryforwards preserved by charter transfer restrictions. **[Fact]** Common stock today has no claim on the Trust property. **[Fact]** The only economic optionality is whether Steel Partners someday uses the shell for a reverse merger or asset drop-down; nothing is disclosed as of 2026-07-21. **[Fact]**
why_market_wrong: The stock trades near two cents on OTC Pink like a dead shell. **[Fact]** The predictive attribute is a dormant asset: charter-level Section 382 protection and Steel Partners' ~40% stake suggest the controlling holder sees future use for the tax attribute even without a announced transaction. **[Inference]** That is real optionality, not a dated catalyst with a known payoff date.

key_assumptions.source: OTC Pink aggregator quotes 2026-07-17 [HUMAN REVIEW: confirm official close]
[Assumption] modest shell optionality re-rating; no disclosed transaction
Q1 2026 IR press release; not additive equity NAV
Q1 2026 IR press release; secured, senior to equity

open_questions: Confirm OTC Pink closing price and shares outstanding against next filed 10-Q.
Reconcile unaudited IR press releases against FY2025 10-K once filed on EDGAR.
Monitor Steel Partners for reverse-merger, asset contribution, going-private, or Form 15 signals.
Confirm post-2025-05-20 buyers have no TPHGreenwich Trust claim (non-transferable interests).
